data.body: Design constraint discovered while implementing: referencing an if:-SKIPPED step's outputs RAISES ExpressionError (executor._resolve_one: 'step did not produce output'), so a bare if:-on-prompt + downstream reconcile-reads-verify won't work. The faithful short-circuit is a BRANCH on steps.precheck.outputs.run_verify: the THEN arm runs verify(prompt)->reconcile(uses); the ELSE arm passes through precheck's deterministic FAIL verdict. Each arm references only steps that run in it (no skipped-output refs). This still guarantees no-LLM-call on precheck failure (the AC intent). Also: the engine AUTO-INJECTS ticket_id/ticket_context/repo_path for prompt steps (ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS), so the verify step needs no 'with'. AC#1 wording updated from literal 'if:' to construct-accurate 'branch short-circuit'.
